steps.title: Identify the defining contrast
steps.content: The passage contrasts two scenarios using “But”: the absence of old friends “one can endure with equanimity,” versus a momentary separation that is “almost unbearable.” This contrast shows that “endure” and “unbearable” are opposites—if something is unbearable (cannot be accepted), then something you can endure must mean you can accept it.

steps.title: Match the meaning
steps.content: ”Endure” means to accept or bear something difficult calmly. Choice C, “tolerate,” directly expresses this meaning—the ability to handle and accept difficulty without distress. The other three describe unrelated concepts: emotional reactions (regret) or unrelated actions (persist, encourage). That matches choice C — C is correct.
